summary: Iteration 2 — validated all newly-added GEMORA admin features. Backend: 38/39 pytest passing (97%); all 12 new endpoints (low-stock, staff auto-invite, delete user/order, purge orders, audit-log) verified with data assertions. Frontend: after fixing an async-useEffect cleanup bug that was crashing 8 admin pages during route changes, all 12 sidebar nav links now navigate correctly (URL updates + main content renders). Owner-only UI controls (purge, per-row delete, low-stock card, temp password field) all present. Verify chain confirmed working — public_key stable across restarts (persisted ED25519 key in env from iteration_1 fix is holding).

minor.issue: Flaky in full-suite run — test_20 fails with 'Reservation lost for unit …' about 50% of the time. The reservation is created at cart-add but appears not-found by mock_pay a moment later, even though test_21 (dispatch) which follows still succeeds. Suspect: reservation is being cleared by a later cart_add re-attempt or the unique index behavior interacts with re-adds. Not blocking (dispatch/vault/revoke still pass); recorded as flaky. This same test also failed in iteration_1 due to a different (key) cause.

Purge flips ALL sold units to available, including units belonging to orders that were NOT in the purge scope in the future (currently there is only one scope: all). Also, purge does not clean db.certificates.activated flags → post-purge those units are technically 'available' but their certificates remain marked activated+sold_to_user_id. Not a bug today because purge is owner-only reset, but worth flagging.

ui_bugs.component: 8 admin pages (AdminUsers, AdminCerts, AdminOrders, AdminQueries, AdminConsultations, AdminTeam, AdminProducts, AdminCategories, AdminInventory)
ui_bugs.issue: FIXED by testing agent — pattern `useEffect(fnReturningPromise, deps)` caused React 'destroy is not a function' TypeError on unmount, crashing every subsequent admin page navigation. Replaced with `useEffect(() => { fn(); }, deps)`.

action_items: Consider fixing the mock-pay 'Reservation lost' flaky failure (see backend/minor) — reproduce by running the pytest suite twice; test_20 sometimes passes, sometimes 409s. Not user-visible but breaks CI determinism.

Optional: audit_log() should record more mutations (e.g., product create/update, category ops) to make the audit trail exhaustive as advertised. Currently it covers only the 5 flows explicitly wrapped.
Optional: on server startup, if ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY_HEX is empty, auto-write a fresh key to backend/.env instead of silently using an ephemeral one. Would remove the iteration_1 footgun entirely.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2036 lines — split into routers (auth, catalog, verify, cart, orders, admin, consultation) + core/{crypto,security,db,seed}.py. Currently every new admin endpoint compounds the file's cognitive load.

AdminUsers/AdminOrders/AdminCerts/AdminTeam/AdminProducts/AdminCategories/AdminInventory/AdminQueries/AdminConsultations all shared the same async-useEffect anti-pattern (`useEffect(fnReturningPromise, deps)` → React sees the Promise and calls it as cleanup → 'destroy is not a function'). Adopt a lint rule (react-hooks/exhaustive-deps + a custom check) to catch this in future PRs.

audit_log() best-effort try/except swallows exceptions — good for the mutation not to fail, but a fully-lost audit trail with no alert defeats the point. Consider a stderr warning + optional in-memory ring buffer as fallback.
admin_delete_user() lets an owner delete a customer whose orders still exist; the orders now point to a ghost user_id. Consider soft-delete or cascade/anonymize.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_gemora_backend.py (updated: signup now requires phone+OTP; test rewritten to include OTP dev flow)

success_rate.frontend: 100% for admin nav (12/12), 100% for admin owner-only UI controls (purge/delete/low-stock/temp-password all render)

test_credentials: Owner admin@gemora.in/admin@1234 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Test staff auto-generated by test_03: any email `staff_<hex>@example.com`, temp password returned in response.
seed_data_creation: Test suite POST /api/dev/seed then adds throwaway staff + customer + orders + reservations under TEST_/staff_/del_ prefixes. Purge-orders test wipes db.orders; the suite handles re-seeding as needed within test_10.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v -n 0` (serial — pytest.ini uses -n 2 --dist loadscope which races the two files against each other and breaks class-scoped state). Frontend: owner sidebar nav testids are `admin-nav-{to}` where `to` is one of dashboard, products, inventory, certificates, orders, categories, astrologers, consultations, queries, broadcast (NOT 'whatsapp'), team, users. Note that WhatsApp nav item uses to='broadcast'.
bug_reported_by_user_status: RESOLVED. The 'admin sidebar shows names but clicking navigates nowhere' bug had TWO root causes: (1) NavLink relative paths (fixed by main agent — now `/admin/${to}`), AND (2) async-useEffect cleanup crash in 9 admin page components (fixed by testing agent in this iteration). Both fixes are needed for the flow to work.
